body:
```
Title: Package two independent rough Heston reference pricers into bin/ and verify price agreement
```

## Goal

Vendor two existing, independent rough Heston reference implementations into the `arb4j/bin/` directory (no Java reimplementation required) and add a unit test that runs both, compares their prices on a shared parameter grid, and fails if they disagree beyond a set tolerance. The resulting verified prices then serve as the ground truth against which the PaDE-expansion-based pricer is validated.

## Reference Implementation 1 — sigurdroemer/rough_heston (MATLAB)

**Repo:** https://github.com/sigurdroemer/rough_heston  
**Language:** MATLAB  
**Method:** Fourier inversion (Lord–Kahl optimal contour) + Diethelm (2004) Adams scheme for the Volterra integral equation of the characteristic function  
**Entry point:** `get_started.m`  
**License:** MIT

### Tasks
- [ ] Clone or subtree `sigurdroemer/rough_heston` into `bin/rough_heston_matlab/`
- [ ] Write a thin MATLAB CLI wrapper `bin/rough_heston_matlab/price_european.m` that:
  - Accepts parameters via command-line args or a JSON/CSV file: `S0, K, T, H, lambda, theta, nu, rho, r`
  - Prints a single line: `call_price put_price` to stdout
- [ ] Verify the wrapper reproduces the `get_started.m` examples exactly

## Reference Implementation 2 — jgatheral/RationalRoughHeston (Python/R)

**Repo:** https://github.com/jgatheral/RationalRoughHeston  
**Language:** Python (Jupyter notebooks) + R  
**Method:** Rational approximations to the rough Heston fractional Riccati ODE (Gatheral & Radoičić 2019, 2023)  
**License:** check repo

### Tasks
- [ ] Clone or subtree `jgatheral/RationalRoughHeston` into `bin/rough_heston_gatheral/`
- [ ] Write a thin Python CLI wrapper `bin/rough_heston_gatheral/price_european.py` that:
  - Accepts the same parameter set as Implementation 1 (via argparse or JSON)
  - Prints a single line: `call_price put_price` to stdout
- [ ] Verify the wrapper reproduces at least one notebook example exactly

## Shared Parameter Grid (Spot Checks)

Both implementations must agree to within `tol = 1e-4` (absolute) on all of the following:

| # | S0  | K   | T   | H    | lambda | theta | nu  | rho  | r   |
|---|-----|-----|-----|------|--------|-------|-----|------|-----|
| 1 | 100 | 100 | 1.0 | 0.10 | 2.0    | 0.04  | 0.3 | -0.7 | 0.0 |
| 2 | 100 | 110 | 1.0 | 0.10 | 2.0    | 0.04  | 0.3 | -0.7 | 0.0 |
| 3 | 100 | 90  | 1.0 | 0.10 | 2.0    | 0.04  | 0.3 | -0.7 | 0.0 |
| 4 | 100 | 100 | 0.5 | 0.05 | 1.5    | 0.02  | 0.4 | -0.5 | 0.0 |
| 5 | 100 | 100 | 2.0 | 0.15 | 3.0    | 0.06  | 0.8 | -0.9 | 0.0 |

## Unit Test

Add `src/test/java/arb/roughheston/RoughHestonReferenceAgreementTest.java`:

```java
@ParameterizedTest
@CsvFileSource(resources = "/rough_heston_spot_checks.csv", numLinesToSkip = 1)
void twoReferenceImplementationsAgree(
        double S0, double K, double T,
        double H, double lambda, double theta, double nu, double rho, double r) {
    double tol = 1e-4;
    double callMatlab   = RoughHestonBinRunner.price("matlab",  S0, K, T, H, lambda, theta, nu, rho, r);
    double callGatheral = RoughHestonBinRunner.price("gatheral", S0, K, T, H, lambda, theta, nu, rho, r);
    assertEquals(callMatlab, callGatheral, tol,
        "Reference pricers disagree at H=" + H + " T=" + T + " K=" + K);
}
```

`RoughHestonBinRunner` is a small Java helper that shells out to the appropriate `bin/` wrapper script and parses the stdout price.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Both wrappers run headlessly from a CI environment (no GUI MATLAB license required — use MATLAB Runtime / MCR, or replace with Octave if MCR is unavailable)
- [ ] All 5 spot-check rows pass at `tol = 1e-4`
- [ ] The test class is tagged `@Tag("reference")` so it can be excluded from the fast unit-test suite and run only when needed
- [ ] Once both implementations agree, a second test class `RoughHestonPaDeValidationTest` re-uses the same CSV to compare the PaDE-expansion pricer against the verified reference prices

## Notes

- Do **not** reimplement the Adams scheme or Fourier inversion in Java. The point is to use these codebases as-is.
- Rough Heston only. No rBergomi, no quadratic rough Heston variants.
- If MATLAB Runtime is impractical in CI, `sigurdroemer/rough_heston` may be ported to Octave (it is simple MATLAB with no toolbox dependencies) or swapped for the Python implementation at `hagerpa/roughprix` (https://github.com/hagerpa/roughprix), which uses the same Fourier + Adams approach in pure Python.
title: Package two independent rough Heston reference pricers into bin/ and verify price agreement
